The liver is an essential organ that plays a crucial role in the muscular system by providing nutrients and supporting muscle function. It helps in the breakdown of [protein], [fat], and [carbohydrates] to supply energy to [muscle] tissues. The liver also produces [enzymes] that aid in the digestion of [fats] and [proteins], which are necessary for [muscle] growth and [recovery]. Additionally, it regulates the [levels] of [hormones] that influence [muscle] metabolism and [recovery]. The liver ensures that [muscle] cells have enough [nutrients] to function properly. It also helps in [reducing] the [amount] of [toxins] that affect [muscle] performance. The liver supports [muscle] contractions by maintaining [proper] [fluid] balance. Finally, it works with [muscle] groups to [optimize] [performance] and [recovery].
